title: dryopteris atrorubens
intro: Dryopteris atrorubens, also known as the Black-stemmed wood fern, is a species of fern native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Dryopteris atrorubens include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as tea, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory infections, sore throats, and skin conditions.
